title: astragalus vesicarius proles leucanthus
intro: Astragalus vesicarius proles leucanthus, also known as White Milk Vetch, is a plant species in the legume family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and hairy stems. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of White Milk Vetch include relieving congestion, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. White Milk Vetch can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
